turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've had a persistent cough for about five days now, and it just won't seem to go away. It started out dry but has been producing yellowish-green sputum lately, even after I've been taking OTC dextromethorphan to try and suppress it.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm getting really frustrated because nothing seems to be helping. I'm worried that this is going to take a lot longer to get over than I had hoped, and it's already affecting my work - I've missed three days of teaching now and I don't know how much more I can keep doing this.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: When I'm having an episode with the coughing, I also get a bit short of breath and wheezy. It feels like my chest is tight and it's harder for me to catch my breath, especially when I'm trying to talk or move around.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: Yes, actually, last night was particularly bad - I woke up drenched in sweat, so much that I had to change my pajamas. It was the fourth night in a row like this.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. As I mentioned, last night I woke up with night sweats, soaking through my pajamas for the fourth time now. So, it seems to be related more to nighttime than anything else.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: It seems to radiate from my chest and then moves up into my throat when I'm coughing. The tightness in my chest feels like it's centered there, but it spreads outwards when I'm having a bad episode with the coughing.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: To be honest, no, it doesn't seem to radiate that far. It's usually just in my chest and throat when I'm having a bad episode with the coughing. But I have been feeling really tired and fatigued lately, even when I'm not having an episode, so maybe that's connected somehow?

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: No, there isn't any numbness or tingling in my arms when I'm experiencing the coughing and tightness. But to be honest, with how tired I've been feeling lately, even when I'm not having an episode, I don't know if that's related or just a separate thing altogether.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: No, there isn't any numbness or tingling in my arms when I'm experiencing the coughing and tightness. Like I mentioned before, with how tired I've been feeling lately, even when I'm not having an episode, I don't know if that's related or just a separate thing altogether.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: No, I haven't noticed any swelling in my ankles, feet, or hands. To be honest, I've been so focused on trying to get rid of this cough and dealing with the fatigue that I haven't really paid attention to those kinds of symptoms.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about my feet feeling tighter or anything like that. To be honest, with how tired and preoccupied I've been lately, I haven't really paid attention to things like that. But I do know that I've had a low-grade fever on and off, around 38.2 degrees Celsius, which has been intermittent but seems worse in the evenings.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't really experienced any stomach issues or nausea during the episodes with coughing. But, if I'm being completely honest, since taking that dextromethorphan for the past week, I have had a bit of an off feeling in my stomach, almost like it's just been slightly queasy when I wake up in the morning. It's not severe or anything, but it is noticeable and seems to be related to the medication, I think.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: No, to be honest, I haven't really experienced any nausea or upset stomach during the episodes with coughing. But like I mentioned earlier, since taking that dextromethorphan for the past week, I've had a bit of an off feeling in my stomach when I wake up in the morning, almost like it's been slightly queasy.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: I have had a low-grade fever, around 38.2 degrees Celsius, which has been intermittent but seems worse in the evenings. And yes, I've also been experiencing night sweats, soaking through my pajamas for the fourth time now. It's been really concerning and frustrating, to be honest.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: Actually, yes - I have noticed a decrease in my appetite over the past 5 days, which is quite unusual for me. Normally, I'm always eating well to keep up with my energy levels, but since this cough started, I just haven't been hungry like I usually am.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: I'd say my diet has definitely been affected by this illness. Since my appetite decreased, I've been eating lighter meals and trying to stick to things that are easy on my stomach. As for exercise, I haven't been able to do much of anything physical lately, which is really frustrating for me because I'm an elementary school teacher and I love being active with the kids. I usually try to get in a morning run or some yoga before work, but since this cough started, even just getting out of bed has felt like a chore.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been pretty inactive lately. As an elementary school teacher, I usually try to get in some physical activity before work, but since this cough started, even just getting out of bed has felt like a chore. Normally, I'd aim for at least 30 minutes of exercise per day, whether it's a run or some yoga, but lately, that's just not been possible.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: To be honest, my appetite has really decreased over the past 5 days, so I haven't been eating as much as I normally would. Normally, I'd have a big breakfast, maybe some oatmeal or scrambled eggs, and then a light lunch, like a sandwich or salad. But since this cough started, even thinking about food can be overwhelming, so I've just been sticking to very light meals, like toast or plain crackers. Sometimes I'll eat something just because it's easy to manage with my energy levels, but I haven't really had much of an appetite for anything substantial.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My mother has asthma, and that's something that's been a concern for me since I was younger. I've always tried to be mindful of my own respiratory health because of her condition.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: Yes, my mother has asthma, and that's been a concern for me since I was younger. She's always been very diligent about managing it, but seeing how it affects her makes me worry about my own respiratory health even more.
